title: Build a Production-Grade Pokedex in Go
description: Go learners ready to integrate commands, HTTP, typed JSON, state, caching, concurrency, cancellation, persistence, security, testing, packaging, and operations into one cumulative user-facing product. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate a cumulative accessible repl pokedex using pokéapi resource, pagination, pokemon, and location-area contracts through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate go ownership, typed json, reusable http clients, fair-use caching, duplicate suppression, goroutines, cancellation, persistence, and recovery through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate original deterministic browser models plus controlled go compiler, httptest, race, fuzz, filesystem, signal, package, and release transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five distinct stakeholder projects and a cumulative performance defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Define learner, field user, decision, command sequence, accepted latency, failure consequence, and observable value before coding.
misconceptions: Printing one Pokemon record proves the product is useful.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define learner, field user, decision, command sequence, accepted latency, failure consequence, and observable value before coding.

competencies.statement: Specify command names, argument grammar, whitespace policy, help text, aliases, unknown-command behavior, and stable output contracts.
misconceptions: A REPL can infer any command grammar from arbitrary user text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Specify command names, argument grammar, whitespace policy, help text, aliases, unknown-command behavior, and stable output contracts.

competencies.statement: Model start, prompt, read, parse, dispatch, result, continue, EOF, quit, cancellation, and cleanup as explicit session states.
misconceptions: A for loop alone defines a complete REPL lifecycle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model start, prompt, read, parse, dispatch, result, continue, EOF, quit, cancellation, and cleanup as explicit session states.

competencies.statement: Keep prompts, progress, errors, and results understandable without color, animation, pointer input, or fragile terminal width.
misconceptions: Terminal software is automatically accessible because it contains only text.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep prompts, progress, errors, and results understandable without color, animation, pointer input, or fragile terminal width.

competencies.statement: Define runnable happy, changed, invalid, EOF, timeout, cancellation, and recovery cases plus exact stakeholder evidence.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define runnable happy, changed, invalid, EOF, timeout, cancellation, and recovery cases plus exact stakeholder evidence.

competencies.statement: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.
misconceptions: A small learning repository does not need baseline or rollback evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect revision, branch, worktree, ignored files, configuration, tests, fixtures, secrets, licenses, and recovery anchor before editing.

competencies.statement: Represent omitted, null, zero, empty, and unknown states according to the endpoint contract instead of collapsing them.

competencies.statement: Decide which not-found, transient, malformed, and canceled outcomes may be cached and for how long.

competencies.statement: Bound entries and bytes, define eviction, expose hit, miss, stale, eviction, and bypass counters, and test each transition.
